Finding: Stale recovery could replace a newer authoritative generation
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Recovery now verifies both prior manifest SHA-256 and generation ID before authority-changing commit; stale recovery fails closed and must be handled as a fork/export.

Finding: Cancellation could mask a fatal branch
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Typed SAFE_STOPPED_DEGRADED status preserves fatal branch diagnostics and forced termination provenance.

Finding: Infinite telemetry unbounded on disk
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Segmented telemetry with bounded segment retention/size, durable segment manifest, and streaming-oriented finalization replaces one unbounded JSONL stream.

Finding: Device-aware branch scheduling lacked memory admission
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Branch admission considers estimated per-branch memory and reserve fraction with device concurrency caps and CPU fallback. Physical-device calibration remains a target-hardware gate.

Finding: PPO training transition semantics differed from deployed CALO
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Native v5.9 defines raw global policy action plus explicit environmental interventions; training and runtime share aligned clocks/memory/lane/group/recovery semantics. Seeded 3-transition parity regression matches population, pbest, RNG, epsilon, credits, rewards and executed operators exactly.
Evidence: policy_schema.py / optimizer.py / training.py / v5.9 parity regression

Finding: Forced recovery credited overridden PPO operator
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Executed operator/intervention is recorded separately; forced recovery is credited as actual recovery rather than to the overridden sampled operator.

Finding: Training/runtime reward definitions differed
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Both paths use shared calculate_reward() coefficients and semantics; seeded transition parity checks reward equality.

Finding: Champion/Base pooled heterogeneous raw objectives; synthetic evidence could promote Base
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Synthetic evidence is Training-Champion screening only. Deployable Base requires exact real-ORPD development evidence; real-case objectives are formulation-specific normalized and final selection is common-bundle/order-independent.

Finding: Feasible episode meant any feasible population member
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Metrics separate any_feasible_found_rate, final_best_feasible_rate and final feasible ratio; Base eligibility uses final-best feasibility per real development case.

Finding: Real policy development did not inherit exact ORPD formulation
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Deployable evidence loads an exact ExperimentConfig including objective, variables, robust scenarios, PF options and constraint tolerances.

Finding: Historical trajectories were lossy 24D/postprocessed supervision
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: v5.9 records full 32D raw policy state/action distributions and separately records executed controller interventions/actions. Legacy 24D data remains explicitly legacy/downweighted rather than silently exact.

Finding: Historical transfer compatibility key too weak
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Full scientific formulation fingerprint covers case, variable manifest, objective, PF, robust/scenario transforms, tolerances, repair/schema identity.

Finding: Repair telemetry double-counted or bypassed depending evaluator/cache
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Repair occurs through one accounting authority before cache/evaluation; cached and uncached regression gives identical candidate/coordinate counts.

Finding: Feasibility tolerances effectively inconsistent/near-zero
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Versioned ConstraintToleranceConfig is propagated through CPU/accelerator/scientific fingerprints with per-constraint tolerances.

Finding: Complex CALO needs ablation for causal publication claims
v5.9 Status: PROTOCOL REQUIREMENT / NOT A CODE BUG
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Ablation infrastructure remains; final papers must predeclare and run ablations under equal FE/seeds/evaluator. v5.9 does not fabricate ablation evidence.

Finding: Evaluation cache can affect runtime comparisons
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: FE accounting remains request-fair; runtime claims must disclose cache policy/hit rates.

Finding: N-1 bundles omitted intact base system
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Branch/generator N-1 builders include intact base (P0) plus selected single contingencies by default.

Finding: ANGMIN/ANGMAX validated but not enforced
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Branch angle-difference violation implemented with MATPOWER-compatible 0/0 and ±360 sentinel semantics across CPU and accelerated constraint evaluators.

Finding: Accelerator parity ignored configured PowerFlowOptions
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Parity problem/fingerprint now includes exact objective, variable config, robust config, scenarios, PF options and tolerance schema.

Finding: Parity sample coverage too weak
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Deterministic battery covers center/bounds/corners/alternating plus random samples; parity compares objective/constraint components, feasibility, convergence, bus types, voltage magnitude/angle and scenarios. Physical hardware gate still required.

Finding: Objective config allowed negative/nonfinite weights and bad scales
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: ObjectiveConfig fails fast on nonfinite/negative weights, nonpositive scales and invalid multiobjective zero-weight definitions.

Finding: Yf/Yt used dense temporary allocation
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Branch current matrices are assembled directly as sparse triplets/CSR.

Finding: Co-located generator Q dispatch convention was implicit
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Capability-proportional post-allocation is explicitly documented as deterministic reporting/limit accounting, not unique physical AVR dispatch.

Finding: Independent validation wording overstated full ORPD independence
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Validation is explicitly independent AC power-flow cross-validation using configured PF options; it is not claimed as a separate complete ORPD reimplementation.

Finding: MATPOWER status/self-loop compatibility assumptions
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Positive status means online, nonpositive offline; self-loop topology errors apply to in-service branches.

Finding: MONTE_CARLO and LOAD_UNCERTAINTY were methodologically indistinct
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Load uncertainty uses deterministic stratified scenarios; Monte Carlo uses seeded random scenarios.

Finding: Load uncertainty is simplified global scaling
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Retained as system-wide aggregate load-scaling model; not claimed as spatially correlated nodal uncertainty.

Finding: Renewable uncertainty is simplified local net-load model
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Retained and explicitly scoped as simplified renewable net-load uncertainty, not a full inverter-control stochastic model.

Finding: Non-inferiority was descriptive margin check, not formal test
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: One-sided paired Wilcoxon/sign fallback against declared margin plus Holm correction; formal gate is case-wise and comparator-by-case.

Finding: Sidebar and guided workflow order disagreed
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Canonical order places Portfolio Manager immediately after Algorithms, then CALO Intelligence, Scenarios, Experiment; experiment gate requires all final prerequisites.

Finding: Scientific configs relied too much on GUI validation
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Objective, PF, robust, variable and tolerance configs fail fast at object boundary for finite/domain constraints.

Finding: Auxiliary branch matrices densified
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Direct sparse Yf/Yt construction.

Finding: CALO control remains partly host/Python
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Not rewritten blindly because trajectory parity is a scientific gate. Accelerator evaluator is available; deeper device residency requires separately qualified seeded parity.

Finding: Broad exception suppression could hide scientific authority failures
v5.9 Status: RESOLVED FOR SILENT-BROAD PATTERN / CONTINUED HARDENING
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: AST audit finds zero broad Exception/BaseException/bare handlers whose sole action is pass/continue/return. Critical authority paths use typed/logged failure handling.

Finding: Post-generation audit found default PolicyQualifier.run referenced optional config instead of resolved qconfig
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Non-inferiority margin now always comes from validated qconfig; thresholds receive additional finite/domain validation.

Finding: Post-generation parity audit found branch-angle device indexing could mix scenario indices with bus endpoints
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Device-resident angle delta indexes bus-angle matrix directly by topology-invariant fidx/tidx; scenario indices apply only to scenario-varying status/limits.

Finding: Post-generation audit found scientific cross-run batch signature could be too weak
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Batch signature is based on canonical full scientific problem fingerprint plus device/dtype.

Finding: Post-generation audit found v5.9 freeze did not initially cover several new scientific modules
Resolution / Scientific Treatment: Freeze scope expanded to parity audit, formulation fingerprint, experience repository, core case/branch modules, evaluation budget/seed manager, scientific scripts and relevant workflow panels.
